question_text: A beaker contains 200g of water at 25°C. You add 100g of water at 95°C to the beaker. Without any heat loss to surroundings, calculate the approximate final temperature of the mixture. (Assume specific heat capacity of water is 4.2 J/g°C and both quantities of water have the same specific heat capacity.)
explanation: Heat lost by hot water = heat gained by cold water. Using Q = mcΔT: 100 × 4.2 × (95 - T) = 200 × 4.2 × (T - 25). Solving: 9500 - 100T = 200T - 5000, so 14500 = 300T, giving T ≈ 48°C. This shows thermal equilibrium where the final temperature is closer to the larger mass of cooler water.
